phrases.intent_en: Ask where the main campus entrance is
phrases.text_local: L'entrée principale du campus, c'est par où ?
phrases.pronunciation: lahn-TRAY prann-see-PAHL doo KAHM-poo say par OO
phrases.cultural_note: McGill's main gate on Sherbrooke Street opens onto a grand central path leading up to the Arts Building. The campus is open to visitors.

responses.english: On Sherbrooke Street, through the gates.
responses.phonetic: soor lah ROO sher-BROOK par lay GREE-yuh
responses.local: Sur la rue Sherbrooke, par les grilles.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if the Redpath Museum is open to the public
phrases.text_local: Le musée Redpath est ouvert au grand public ?
phrases.pronunciation: luh moo-ZAY red-PATH ay oo-VAIR oh grahn poo-BLEEK
phrases.cultural_note: The Redpath Museum is Canada's oldest natural history museum and free to visit. It houses ancient Egyptian mummies and dinosaur fossils.

responses.english: Yes, it is open on weekdays.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to find a good café near campus
phrases.text_local: Y'a-tu un bon café sympa pas trop loin du campus ?
phrases.pronunciation: yah-TOO un bon kah-FAY sam-PAH pah troh LWAH doo KAHM-poo
phrases.cultural_note: Milton Street and the surrounding area is packed with student cafés. The neighbourhood is bilingual with both French and English students.

responses.english: There are several cafés on University Street.
responses.phonetic: eel ee ah ploo-ZYUR kah-FAY soor lah ROO oo-nee-vair-see-TAY
responses.local: Il y a plusieurs cafés sur la rue University.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for directions to the Roddick Gates
phrases.text_local: Les portes Roddick, c'est dans quelle direction ?
phrases.pronunciation: lay PORT ROD-ik say dahn kel dee-rek-SYON
phrases.cultural_note: The Roddick Gates at the corner of McGill College and Sherbrooke are a famous Montréal landmark. The carillon bell tower rings on the quarter hour.

responses.english: Straight ahead, at the bottom of the hill.
responses.phonetic: too DRWAH ahn BAH duh lah koh-LEEN
responses.local: Tout droit, en bas de la colline.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about the view of the city from campus
phrases.text_local: On a une belle vue sur le centre-ville depuis le campus ?
phrases.pronunciation: on ah OON bel VOO soor luh sahn-truh-VEEL duh-PWEE luh KAHM-poo
phrases.cultural_note: McGill's campus sits at the foot of Mount Royal, offering views down McGill College Avenue toward the St. Lawrence River on clear days.

responses.english: Go to the upper field, the view is nice.
responses.phonetic: ah-LAY soor luh tay-RAHN soo-pay-RYUR lah VOO eh BEL
responses.local: Allez sur le terrain supérieur, la vue est belle.
